demographics.living_situation: lives at home with her partner

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Sudden pain in my right calf and I am terrified of getting a blood clot.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The calf pain came on out of nowhere a couple of hours ago and I'm pregnant with a condition that makes me clot more easily, so I'm scared it's a clot.

symptom_narrative.onset: about two hours ago, came on suddenly
symptom_narrative.duration: a couple of hours and still there
symptom_narrative.character: started as a dull ache and turned into a sharp cramping pain
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: uncomfortable, worse when I walk on it
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: there most of the time, sharper when I put weight on it and walk
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: resting in bed with my leg up seems to ease it a bit
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no shortness of breath, no chest pain, no coughing — mostly I'm just really anxious about it being a clot
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no, I haven't had calf pain like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I've been resting in bed because I don't want to make it worse; I can still get around the house

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a blood condition where my body makes antibodies that make me clot more easily — I think they call it antiphospholipid or 'sticky blood'
past_medical_history.how_managed: I take a low-dose aspirin every day, especially now I'm pregnant

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: one early miscarriage in the past
past_medical_history.how_managed: that's when they tested me and found the antibodies

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: 9 weeks pregnant; her partner is with her; very anxious about the pregnancy and about clots

communication_style.typical_message_style: worried, seeking reassurance, keeps returning to the fear of a clot and the pregnancy

objective_data_offered.value: the leg doesn't look swollen or red to me and it isn't hot to touch, but I'm not sure I'd know what to look for
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient self-assessment of her own leg — cannot substitute for a clinician's examination or compression ultrasound; a normal-looking calf does not exclude a DVT.

objective_data_offered.value: my breathing feels completely normal, I'm not short of breath at all
